steps.title: Connect verb and condition
steps.content: The phrase should read "is added whenever" because "whenever" introduces the condition under which a leap second is added. No punctuation is needed between the verb phrase and that dependent clause.

steps.title: Reject unnecessary punctuation
steps.content: A inserts an unnecessary comma. B uses a semicolon even though what follows is not an independent clause. C creates a sentence break that leaves the "whenever" clause fragmentary.

steps.title: Use direct connection
steps.content: Added whenever correctly connects the action to the condition without punctuation. The answer is D.
